programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: This episode, meet newlyweds Nikki and Andy. Having merged houses a few years ago, Nikki has grown to hate their three-bed terrace but Andy is not for moving.

programme.title: Location Location Location
programme.desc: This time, we're catching up with two sets of house hunters whose searches were all about family. In 2018, retired couple Sue and Tom made the difficult decision to sell their beloved country cottage in Tom's native Yorkshire and move south to be closer to Sue's grandson Freddie. After their house sold in just six days, they found themselves living in their motorhome- and winter was coming, Phil took them on a voyage of discovery through the east of England, where it was as much about finding the perfect garden as the perfect house. Two years later, Phil has returned to see whether their relocation reaped rewards. In 2019, recently divorced dad Charlie was back living with his parents whilst he searched for a new home near Maidstone. Keen to provide space and stability for his children Rosie and Hugo, green fingered Charlie was looking for a house with a large garden - but a tight budget and stock-poor search area was making his search nigh on impossible. Having lost out on several properties, Charlie was all at sea, and needed Kirstie's help to find safe harbour. A year on, we're back to see whether his family found dry land.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location Australia
programme.desc: Veronica and Bryce are up to their necks in nappies, finding homes for two very different families in two different states. Bryce is on the hunt for a couple of urban hippies in Noosa's spectacular hinterland. Chad and Tahlia are loved-up first time buyers looking for a tree-change so baby daughter Venice can be closer to her grandparents. Problem is, it's got to have the right 'vibe' and Bryce has got to dig deep to find his inner hippie! At the opposite end of the spectrum, Veronica's on a multi-million dollar hunt on Sydney's exclusive lower North Shore. She's trying to find the dream home for a blended family of seven. But big budgets mean big wish lists and like kids in a candy store, this couple wants more, more, more!

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: Steven and Theresa own a dated six-bedroom home which has been on the market for three years with three agents. They've lowered the price from $680,000 to $570,000 and can't really go lower. The market is flooded with more contemporary homes, meaning their house isn't currently very appealing to buyers.

programme.title: Selling Houses Australia
programme.desc: This is an incredibly dated 1970s four-bed home on an elevated block with a steep driveway. This lovely family with two kids are preparing to downsize, but they seem slightly clueless as to why their house won't sell, and it's scaring off the buyers. It's been on the market for 14 months with a hopeless agent that they have replaced. They'll need to lower their expectations as they're currently asking well over the suburb average, even before the team renovates.

programme.title: Love It Or List It Australia
programme.desc: Will Kristin and Di move to a new home with enough land for a big veggie garden and a brood of chooks or will Neale be able to reconfigure their current Queenslander home and make them stay put.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Georgina and Philip are building high with sweeping views across the entire bay. But in what becomes the longest delay ever witnessed on the series, can they muster the financial and emotional will to keep going?

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: This episode, meet newlyweds Nikki and Andy. Having merged houses a few years ago, Nikki has grown to hate their three-bed terrace but Andy is not for moving.

programme.desc: Phil makes his way to an island paradise to visit a unique beach side home and travels to Queenstown for the breathtaking views from a home made of copper.

programme.title: Grand Designs Australia
programme.desc: Tani Richards has some fascinating pastimes. As well as practising yoga and horse riding, she has a passion for all things medieval, including dressing up like Maid Marion for a bit of horse back archery! After selling her and pilot husband Tim's medieval style family home known as the 'Sand Castle' in Perth's City Beach, she has plans to build an 'earth castle' on their 60 acre property north of Perth. But this is not your standard earth castle! Built into the sides of a man-made dam, there are plans for a drawbridge, turret and even a moate. With underground rooms and tunnels, and gothic features and finishes, the footprint is vast- nearly 2000 square metres in total. But Tani's endless modifications, coupled with fallout from the pandemic take a toll on the budget and the timelines- not to mention many of Tani's dreams for her medieval fortress!

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. Five truck owners will compete in a series of gruelling challenges that test their craftsmanship and driving ability to the max. Each episode features unique challenges that test trucks in a way that's never been seen before. The challenges culminate in The Green Hell, a three-mile obstacle course which includes challenges that send trucks flying off jumps, over a mountain of crushed cars and even through a snake-infested swamp. Helping them along the way are four experts in the off-road world - they coach, they build, they fix, but the one thing they can't do is drive. In the end, the truck who finishes is crowned the champion - if they can make it that far. In this episode, an epic line-up features a 2000 horsepower Dodge pick-up competing against a 1971 Land Rover. In a never-before-seen twist on the tug-o-war, the Silo Pulldown dumps a big surprise on the losers. And one challenge hits hard, forcing the medics to rush in.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: There's trouble in the wind for Kurt Shepherd who is struggling with a load causing oversized problems. For Johnny Dowell on the Tanami Track it's too hard one minute and too soft the next.

programme.title: American Pickers
programme.desc: In Michigan, a late collector's home features a stockpile of awesome wheels. And a wide-eyed Mike comes up with a staggering six-figure offer - the largest in American Pickers history - to take home five ultra-rare motorbikes. Meanwhile, Danielle nails a shot-in-the-dark mission for rock'n'roll legend Jack White and then it's up to Robbie and Dave to transport a legendary recording trailer to its new home in Detroit.

programme.title: Storage Wars
programme.desc: Winds in excess of 48km/h batter Desert Hot Springs, and the buyers do their best to keep standing; Rene and Gunther dust off an old locker; Kenny steps up his game.

programme.title: Truck Night In America
programme.desc: This program makes history with the toughest truck obstacle course ever built. America and trucks go together and, since 1925 when the first American factory-produced truck rolled off the line, American owners have been devising ingenious ways to make them faster, stronger, and better. In a never-before-seen twist on the tug-o-war, the Silo Pulldown dumps a big surprise on the losers. And one challenge hits hard, forcing the medics to rush in.

programme.title: Taskmaster
programme.desc: Jo Brand demonstrates an impressive grasp of street slang and David Baddiel angrily wields a sledgehammer.

programme.title: Idiot TV
programme.desc: A series of clips featuring the biggest fools on the planet. It's gold medal buffoonery collected and curated like never before, with rewinds, slow-mo's and close ups.

programme.title: Idiot TV
programme.desc: A series of clips featuring the biggest fools on the planet. It's gold medal buffoonery collected and curated like never before, with rewinds, slow-mo's and close ups.

programme.title: Superstars (WWF)
programme.desc: Featuring the stars of the World Wrestling Federation from wrestling matches and interviews to updates on ongoing feuds.

programme.title: WWE Raw Highlights
programme.desc: Catchup with the latest beat downs and aerial assaults in World Wrestling. Featuring some of the biggest and baddest wrestling superstars straight off the satellite.

programme.title: Aussie Pickers
programme.desc: Join Lucas Callaghan and Adam McDonald, Australia's own pickers, as they scour the country's back roads and barnyards in the quest to turn one man's trash into another man's treasure. Along the way we meet some of Australia's most authentic and colourful characters, whose stories reveal a rich Australian history, and what the pickers find will surprise and astound you.

programme.title: Spectacular Spider-Man
programme.desc: An animated television show that focuses on a sixteen-year-old Peter Parker, and the origins of Spider-Man.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Spawn looks to uncover the truth about his identity and fulfil his destiny.

programme.title: Todd McFarlane's Spawn
programme.desc: Spawn's nightmares reveal that Al Simmons' killer was his mercenary ex-partner, Chapel. CIA chief Jason Wynn tells Chapel to find a cache of stolen guns before they are uncovered and traced back to him.

programme.title: Shipping Wars
programme.desc: Follow independent shippers who have discovered that fortunes can be made by transporting items that traditional carriers won't touch. Each episode of Shipping Wars dives into the cutthroat world of these heavy-duty movers as they battle for the chance to transport the un-shippable. The competition begins with uShip, the world's largest online auction house for independent truckers. From oversized loads and bulky packages to the truly bizarre, these shippers will haul anything if the price is right. From rookie haulers to seasoned veterans, no shipment is too big for these professionals to handle. Marc, Jarrett, Jennifer, Roy and his beloved cat Muffy are back, along with new cast members Chris and Robbie. One of the industry's most unique shippers, Chris's edge in the business comes from his truck, which runs off of a waste vegetable oil system that he invented himself. While he's on the road, his girlfriend and business partner Robbie books loads and maps out routes while taking care of her son, Carter. Don't let their age fool you, this South Carolina couple juggles parenthood, running a successful business, and taking care of their golden retriever, Gix, who is a permanent passenger on the road.

programme.title: Pawn Stars
programme.desc: Take a look inside the doors of a family-run pawnshop in Las Vegas, where three generations of men from the Harrison family, grandfather Richard, son Rick and grandson Corey jointly run the business, with clashing and camaraderie every step of the way. This family lives and breathes the pawning business, using their sharp-eyed skills to carefully assess the value of items their colourful customers bring in, objects ranging from the obscure to the truly historic.

Jon Snow heads north to join the brotherhood of the Night's Watch. Tyrion decides to forgo the trip south with his family, instead joining Jon in the entourage heading to the Wall. Viserys bides his times in hopes of winning back the throne, while Dany focuses her attention on learning how to please her new husband, Drogo.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: At Castle Black, Jon Snow impresses Tyrion at the expense of greener recruits; suspicious that the Lannisters had a hand in Bran's fall, Catelyn covertly follows her husband to King's Landing, where she is intercepted by Petyr Baelish, aka Littlefinger, a shrewd long-time ally and brothel owner; Cersei and Jaime ponder the implications of Bran's recovery.

programme.title: Game of Thrones

Sansa imagines her future as a queen, while Arya envisions a far different future. In Harrenhal, Arya receives a promise from Jaqen H'ghar, one of three prisoners she saved from the Gold Cloaks, while the Night's Watch arrive at the Fist of the First Men, an ancient fortress where they hope to stem the advance of the wildling army.

programme.title: True Blood
programme.desc: It's Samhain, Wicca's greatest holy day, and spirits of the dead surface in Bon Temps, giving Sookie some valuable allies to combat Marnie's newest incarnation. Lafayette's latest medium encounter imperils his relationship with Jesus, and Jason finds confession good for the soul but not the body. Alcide makes a heartfelt appeal to the woman he loves, and Terry receives an unexpected visitor at Merlotte's. Sam and Luna envision a storybook ending for once, and Nan wears out her welcome with Bill and Eric. Spirits of the dead converge on Bon Temps as Sookie and her two favourite vampires battle Marnie's latest incarnation.

programme.title: John From Cincinnati
programme.desc: Early on the sixth day since John's arrival, Butchie Yost is trying to get back in the game. With Kai looking on from a hiding spot on the beach, Butchie attacks a succession of waves, but some spectacular wipe-outs are showing his rust. Before long, though, the lapsed pro starts landing his tricks, and Kai slinks off to her friend Jane's house to pick up his old boards. From the creators behind Deadwood and surf-noir writer Kem Nunn, this Showcase Original Presentation is set in Imperial Beach, California, the last great surf-break before Tijuana. This is a story about the Yosts; a family of surfers whose awesome athletic talents have seemed for generations to come with a curse attached. The Yosts' reign and reputation has been eroded by years of bad luck, addiction and hubris. A stranger named John arrives - and the Yosts' banal existence is lifted into something profound, miraculous and, possibly, universal. The distinctive theme song is by the late Joe Strummer and the Mescaleros.

programme.desc: Butchie takes his surfing re-immersion up a notch by sharing the water with another pro, Sonny Mac. While the two ride intermittent sets, Kai and Shaun drive up to the beach, but Kai suggests they hit another break to give Butchie some space. "In case he gets dusted," Shaun notes. They drive off in the Jeep, and when the two pros step out of the water, Sonny confirms Butchie's impression: His dry spell has ended. From the creators behind Deadwood and surf-noir writer Kem Nunn, this Showcase Original Presentation is set in Imperial Beach, California, the last great surf-break before Tijuana. This is a story about the Yosts; a family of surfers whose awesome athletic talents have seemed for generations to come with a curse attached. The Yosts' reign and reputation has been eroded by years of bad luck, addiction and hubris. A stranger named John arrives - and the Yosts' banal existence is lifted into something profound, miraculous and, possibly, universal. The distinctive theme song is by the late Joe Strummer and the Mescaleros. programme.desc: Tyrion lends Jaime a hand, while Joffrey and Margaery host a breakfast. At Dragonstone, Stannis loses patience with Davos while Ramsay finds a purpose for his pet. North of the Wall, Bran sees where they must go.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Tyrion ponders his options while Tywin extends an olive branch. Sam realises Castle Black isn't safe, and Jon proposes a bold plan. The Hound teaches Arya the way things are. Meanwhile, Daenerys chooses her champion.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Daenerys balances justice and mercy while Jaime tasks Brienne with his honour. Jon secures volunteers while Bran, Jojen, Meera and Hodor stumble on shelter.

programme.desc: Tyrion enlists an unlikely ally, while Daario entreats Daenerys to allow him to do what he does best. Jon's warnings about the Wall's vulnerability fall on deaf ears, and Brienne follows a new lead on the road with Pod.

programme.title: Game of Thrones
programme.desc: Mole's Town receives some unexpected visitors while Littlefinger's motives are questioned. Meanwhile, Ramsay attempts to prove himself to his father and Tyrion's fate is decided.

programme.title: Game of Thrones

In search of the missing Marcus, Alcide's werewolf pack comes after Sam. With Bill away, Jessica enjoys her new freedom by partying with some local college kids, and Jason is visited by the recently turned Reverend Steve Newlin. Terry's PTSD is reignited by an old Iraq War buddy, Patrick, while Alcide turns up at Sookie's to warn her about the recently resurfaced Russell Edgington.

programme.title: John From Cincinnati
programme.desc: Mitch walks down the beach in Mexico, toward an older man sitting in front of a shack. The surfer's former acid hookup, known as the Chemist, has given up his lab and now goes by his real name: Erlemeyer. Mitch isn't looking to trip, but he's hoping the psychotropic guru can help him find some answers to his levitation and Shaun's resurrection. programme.desc: Ziva and Tony spend time together in a hotel as they go undercover, assuming the identities of hired assassins who are now dead. Gibbs soon discovers that his team is not the only one working the case.

programme.desc: The Fugitive Task Force chases a killer who plays a twisted game of cat and mouse with his victims.

programme.desc: While on duty, McGee kills a man who is later discovered to be an undercover police officer. When the director decides to investigate the situation, the NCIS team are forced to prove McGee's innocence.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Babies, Lies And A Resplendent Cannoli
programme.desc: Sheldon is upset at the recent death of science-fiction author Isaac Asimov. Dr. Linkletter is a fan and Dr. Sturgis is interested in his novels. Since Sheldon's late grandfather enjoyed the novels as well, Sheldon talks Connie into joining his book club with the two professors to read "Nightfall". Once the two men find out Dale dumped Connie, they flirt with her and both leave her several voicemails. Meanwhile, Georgie tells Dale about Mandy being pregnant; Georgie is the father and Mandy wants to keep it. Dale later urges Georgie to tell George. Missy has her own disastrous experience babysitting Pastor Jeff's baby, alarming Georgie. In the garage, Georgie finally tells his father the truth.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: A God-Fearin' Baptist And A Hot Trophy Husband
programme.desc: George is shocked and quickly has Georgie tell Mary. Though upset, Mary and George note they did the same when Mary got pregnant with Georgie. Mary informs Connie who is able to convince Mandy to come to dinner with the Coopers; relating how she felt back when Mary got pregnant. The dinner is awkward, with Mary wanting the baby to be raised Baptist and wanting Georgie and Mandy to get married. Mandy and George both think that is a horrible idea while Georgie would be willing to marry her. Mary and George get into an argument about their own marriage. Mandy is thankful for Connie's support though. Mandy later rebuffs Georgie's declaration of love for her. Meanwhile, the truth is hidden from Sheldon and Missy. programme.desc: George is shocked and quickly has Georgie tell Mary. Though upset, Mary and George note they did the same when Mary got pregnant with Georgie. programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden may lose Louis when the social worker finds out that he and Alan aren't actually a couple, but Alan isn't ready to give up without a fight.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden joins a support group for adoptive fathers, and learns it's nice to be away from Alan.

programme.title: Two and a Half Men
programme.desc: Walden worries about Louis and the adoption process when Alan breaks up with the social worker in hopes of getting back together with Lyndsey.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: Uncle Sheldon And A Hormonal Firecracker
programme.desc: Mary and George are still bickering. After Connie tells Sheldon and Missy that Mary isn't pregnant, Missy figures out the truth about Georgie. Mary swears the twins to secrecy. Talking about it without specifics, Mary later feels judged by Pastor Jeff. Sheldon cannot hold it and tells an uninterested Dr. Linkletter, George tells Coach Wilkins, Mary tells Pastor Rob and Missy tells a girl at school. Georgie is upset everyone spread it around and Mandy is further angry with him. Georgie has a heartfelt moment with Missy who believes Georgie will be a good father. George is further irritated Mary bonded with Pastor Rob again over this and listens to him when George had earlier given essentially the same advice; but doesn't tell her why he dislikes Pastor Rob. When nobody attends her Bible Study, a sad Mary calls Pastor Rob who says he always has time for her.

programme.title: Young Sheldon
programme.sub-title: White Trash, Holy Rollers And Punching People
programme.desc: After Missy punches a boy in Sunday school for making fun of her family, Pastor Jeff insists Mary take a leave of absence from working at the church to the objection of both George and Pastor Rob. At home, George disagrees with Mary that Missy should be punished. Pastor Rob stops by and prays with Mary, but Missy comes home interrupting their alone time. Mandy tells Connie her family has disowned her for being pregnant and invites her to accompany her to her medical examination. At the clinic, Georgie showed up despite being uninvited and after some bickering was allowed to stay, while Connie offers to cover the cost of Mandy's prenatal care. George is upset that Mary saw Pastor Rob again in private and storms out of the door. After Sheldon informs President Hagemeyer of his family troubles, Mary snaps at President Hagemeyer over the phone. Missy hugs and supports her mother, later covering for George's absence to her brothers and asking God to bless Mary.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: Beverly looks to set an example for her feuding sons by reuniting Coach Mellor and his estranged older brother. Murray enlists Erica's boyfriend to keep her from having too much fun in college.

programme.title: The Goldbergs
programme.desc: As Adam's campus visit exposes the truth about Erica's college experience, Murray and Beverly are at odds when she looks to start a new career.

programme.desc: Will is reluctant to introduce his ballet dancer boyfriend to Grace. Meanwhile, Jack suggests to his company that he design their Christmas window.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will & Grace try to lure Tina, Will's dad's mistress, away from Will's father by setting her up with another man. Unfortunately, the only man available is Will's gay friend, Larry.

programme.desc: When Frank starts showing a keen interest in Ray's work and is constantly in a cheery mood, everyone questions his radical change in behaviour. Ray soon uncovers the real reason behind Frank's transformation.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Ray and Debra are discouraged when they realise that their sex life isn't what it used to be. They approach Frank and Marie about the situation, however, soon wish they never asked.

programme.desc: A new assistant's incompetence has the Dunder Mifflin staff up in arms, but Michael refuses to fire him, as he's his nephew. Andy tries to ignore Erin's dating of Gabe.

programme.title: The King of Queens

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Daphne enlists Niles to pose as her husband when an unwanted ex-fiance visits from England.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier can't wait to go out with the gorgeous woman Roz found for him, until Bulldog steals her away right under Frasier's nose.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie pairs up the Pawnee Parks employees with their Eagleton counterparts. Meanwhile, Ben and Chris reunite for an accounting project, and Ann reveals her plans for the future.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie tries to calm a minor scandal that gets blown out of proportion. Tom tries to impress a new love interest, while Ben convinces Ron to prepare his last will and testament.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: While visiting Karen's incarcerated husband, Grace re-discovers a handsome high school friend who's also doing time in prison. Despite Will's advice, she's soon dressing up and visiting the inmate.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.sub-title: Dyeing Is Easy, Comedy Is Hard

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Upon drafting their wills, Ray and Debra must decide who would make the best guardians for their children, but their choice doesn't meet with everyone's approval.

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond
programme.desc: Something is clearly up with Debra's sister, Jennifer, when she shows up for a visit with rosary beads and prayer cards as gifts for the kids.

programme.title: Mom
programme.sub-title: Forged Resumes And The Recommended Dosage

programme.title: Mom
programme.sub-title: Kimchi And A Monkey Playing Harmonica
programme.desc: Christy has mixed feelings about Baxter's relationship with his new girlfriend, Candace, while Bonnie and Alvin's romance heats up.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: Having invited the whole office, Andy hopes his local theatre troupe's production of Sweeney Todd will be a chance to impress Erin. Meanwhile, Michael is miffed that his audition for the play went unrewarded.

programme.title: The Office
programme.desc: A cold sore leads to Michael contacting all his old girlfriends telling them he has herpes. Meanwhile, Andy hosts a sex ed forum for the staff.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Things get out of hand as Doug frantically tries to stop a woman who claims to have spent a romantic weekend with him from calling the house.

programme.title: The King of Queens
programme.desc: Carrie gets upset because one of her fellow secretaries "steals her moxie" and gets a promotion, and everything at IPS falls apart after Doug temporarily takes over the office.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: When Jerry starts sleeping with his maid, her work begins to decline, while George decides he wants a nickname.

programme.title: Seinfeld
programme.desc: Jerry and his friends try to escape a Puerto Rican day traffic jam.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie throws Ben a birthday bash but is forced to leave early for an emergency late-night filibuster. Tom tries to make some memories with his new girlfriend, Ron and Donna go hunting, and April reconnects with Andy.

programme.title: Parks and Recreation
programme.desc: Leslie prepares a haunted house with Ben, Ann and Chris for Halloween on the day of the recall vote. Meanwhile, Tom fears the end of Rent-A-Swag and persuades Ron to help save his business.

programme.title: The Drew Carey Show
programme.desc: Kate organizes a salon to impress Mrs. Louder, but it leads to a heated argument when the topic of internet censorship arises.

programme.title: The Drew Carey Show
programme.desc: Drew's jealousy over Kate and Oswald's engagement leads to a compatibility test that causes tension and reveals deep feelings.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam tries to beat Gary at his own game in a grudge basketball game by recruiting Boston Celtics' star Kevin McHale as a substitute bartender at Cheers.

programme.title: Cheers
programme.desc: Sam hires a new bar manager that everyone seems to like, then learns how low Rebecca has sunk in her pursuit of new employment.

programme.title: Frasier
programme.desc: Frasier and Niles struggle to interpret Frasier's reoccurring dream.

programme.desc: Frasier and Niles are ecstatic when their father becomes a good friend with reclusive author TH Houghton.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: A clever charmer named Owen is competing against Jack for a spot in New York's Gay Men's chorus. However, Owen is not all he seems to be.

programme.title: Will & Grace
programme.desc: Will and Grace feel they need more room in their house, so they search for a larger place to live. When they find their ideal place, they illegally sublet their old apartment.

programme.title: Europe's Waterways
programme.sub-title: France - From The Camargue To The Ardeche
programme.desc: Venturing upstream on Europe's waterways, the series discovers five regions of the continent from an unknown perspective and visits people who live from, on and with water. Rivers, canals, lake regions: they have shaped Europe's cultures for centuries - they are a place to live, transport arteries and sources of energy. Five journeys on watercourses broad and narrow lead to Italy, Germany, France, Poland, Holland and Belgium. Along the way: new projects for the future of water in Europe.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Directors and musicians discuss the secrets behind creating some of Lou Reed's best-loved music videos.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: A unique insight into the ideas and vision behind the music videos of BRIT Award-winning rock band Crowded House.

programme.title: Other Voices
programme.desc: A celebration of 20 years of Other Voices - Ireland's longest running music television series. Featuring Sam Fender, Sigrid, Dermot Kennedy, Villagers, Kay Young, Little Simz, John Grant, Orla Gartland and Fontaines DC amongst others.

programme.title: The Canary Islands
programme.sub-title: La Palma - The Galapagos Of Europe
programme.desc: Off the African coast in the middle of the Atlantic, underwater volcanoes raged for millions of years and created a volcanic archipelago: the Canary Islands. Each of them has its own face. This series tells of natural spectacles, unique natural areas and the formation of the volcanic archipelago.

programme.desc: The New Zealand South Island line scales the snow-capped Southern Alps, travels through Maori hunting grounds and finally reaches Christchurch, a city sitting on a volcano.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: In this series, historian Paul Murton sets out to experience life on Scotland's magnificent islands. He uncovers the past and reveals its connections with the present, pointing to all that makes these islands quirky, surprising and beautiful.

programme.title: Branson
programme.desc: The series premiere explores Branson's early years and the influence of his iconoclastic mother. As a dyslexic teenager, he leaves school at age 15 to create a magazine called Student, leading to a mail-order record business and eventually Virgin Records and Virgin megastores.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Directors and musicians reveal the ideas and inspirations that helped create some of American rock band ZZ Top's most memorable music videos.

programme.title: Video Killed the Radio Star
programme.desc: Robert Elms discusses the amazing body of music video work from Bjork. Featuring interviews with directors Chris Cunningham, Michel Gondry and Spike Jonze, and videos All is Full of Love, It's Oh So Quiet and Bachelorette.

programme.title: Too Young To Die
programme.desc: He only lived to be 28 years old. Heath Ledger was a star that didn't really want to be one, opting for roles in small films and declining many major offers.

programme.title: Michael Palin: Travels of a Lifetime
programme.desc: Michael revisits his fourth travel series, Sahara with Michael Palin (2002), a four-episode trek round the Sahara desert.

programme.title: World's Most Scenic Railway Journeys
programme.desc: The Cambrian Line starts in Pwllheli and travels 200km, taking in the fairytale village of Portmeirion, the seaside resort of Aberdovey and the ancient capital of Machynlleth.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Paul Murton continues his odyssey, discovering the secrets of some of the most remote places in Europe. His first journey takes him to the largest island in Britain: the long island of Harris and Lewis. Paul tries his hand at weaving Harris tweed, meets the family behind the world famous Stornoway black pudding and uncovers the remarkable story of how a mysterious medieval chess set was discovered on the remote beach of Uig on Lewis.

programme.title: Grand Tours of the Scottish Islands
programme.desc: Thirty miles from mainland Scotland, there is a chain of low-lying islands that face the full might of the Atlantic Ocean: the Uists and Benbecula. On these islands there seems to be more water than land as a spectacular patchwork of freshwater lochs and pools pockmark the landscape. But it's the unique ecology of these unspoilt islands that makes them so special. Paul's journey sees him try his hand at fishing for wild sea trout, uncover the fascinating story of how South Uist became a missile testing ground, and discover the secrets of the beautiful but deserted tidal island of Vallay.

programme.title: Taken Together: Who Killed Lyric And Eli
programme.desc: Drew Collins confronts someone who may link a suspect to the Evansdale murder scene.

programme.title: Casey Anthony: Where the Truth Lies
programme.desc: Casey Anthony revisits memories from the period when her daughter Caylee went missing. As she slowly pieces together clues, Casey finally admits what she suspects happened that fateful day.

programme.title: The Jinx: Life & Deaths Of Robert Durst
programme.desc: Susan Berman was the daughter of a famous Las Vegas mobster, Davey 'The Jew' Berman, who was a partner with 'Bugsy' Siegel in The Flamingo Hotel. Robert Durst's closest friend since they attended UCLA together, she had a mobster's mentality and matching code of ethics; people said she would do anything for a friend. Since Kathie Durst's disappearance two decades earlier, Robert Durst had been the subject of media and public scrutiny, and she had become his spokesperson to the media. Some say that Robert and Susan kept each other's secrets, and that if anyone knew what happened to Kathie, it would be Susan.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo meets the craftsmen behind the Victorian furniture trade, discovers how George Bradshaw helped save Britain's canal heritage, and sees Shakespeare as a 19th-century railway tourist.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo travels to the manufacturing centres of the Midlands. He learns how the railways helped to make Birmingham the pen-making capital of the world, hears the chilling tale of one of 19th-century Britain's most notorious murderers and samples the delicacies concocted in a Victorian kitchen at Shugborough Hall.

programme.title: Icons Unearthed: Star Wars
programme.desc: Marcia Lucas, Oscar-winning film editor and ex-wife of George Lucas, sits down for her first-ever on camera interview and provides unique insight into the rise of Lucasfilm.

programme.title: Artist to Icon
programme.desc: Features interviews with Whitney Houston's The Bodyguard director Mick Jackson, musician Wayne Linsey, The Preacher's Wife producer Elliot Abbott, plus many more.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: The musical fallout from the night disco died, a rock God breathes life into Tina Turner's career and we explore the otherworldly occurrences in Rick Rubin's recording studio.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Willie Nelson gets over on the IRS, the overlooked band that may have invented punk music and we look into the Frank Sinatra song that has spawned a dozen murders.

programme.title: Landscape Artist of the Year
programme.desc: In this 'best-of' episode we get to observe the most unusual artistic techniques, experience the challenges of painting 'en plein air', and indulge in stunning British landscapes.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.sub-title: Ken Stott, Mike Leigh, Lydia Leonard
programme.desc: In this seventh heat, nine artists are challenged to create a portrait of one of three celebrities: director Mike Leigh and actors Ken Stott and Lydia Leonard.

programme.title: The Man Who Stole The Scream
programme.desc: This gripping story details the 1994 theft of Edvard Munch's The Scream during the Winter Olympics, featuring the chase by authorities and Scotland Yard, focusing on the convicted thief Pal Enger.

programme.title: Master of Light
programme.desc: George Anthony Morton is a classical painter who spent ten years in federal prison for dealing drugs. While incarcerated, he nurtured his craft and unique artistic ability. Since his release, he is doing everything he can to defy society's unlevel playing field and tackle the white-dominant art world. Now, he is on a journey back to his hometown of Kansas City, where he tries to heal the broken relationship with his mother and paint family members in the style of the Dutch Old Masters. Going back home forces George to face his past in his quest to rewrite the script of his life and discover the contribution of African influences to the realist tradition.

programme.title: Christian Hook: Painting the Invisible
programme.desc: Inspired by his fascination with science, artist Christian Hook embarks on a challenge to paint the relationship between two people.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Portillo travels to the manufacturing centres of the Midlands. programme.desc: The musical fallout from the night disco died, a rock God breathes life into Tina Turner's career and we explore the otherworldly occurrences in Rick Rubin's recording studio.

programme.title: Music's Greatest Mysteries
programme.desc: Willie Nelson gets over on the IRS, the overlooked band that may have invented punk music and we look into the Frank Sinatra song that has spawned a dozen murders.

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: In this eighth heat, nine artists are challenged to create a portrait of one of three celebrities: actors Deborah Findlay and Wunmi Mosaku, and actor and comedian Omid Djalili.

programme.title: Windsor Castle Fire 30 Years On
programme.desc: This dramatic documentary reveals the amazing personal stories behind the disastrous Windsor Castle fire that ignited 30 years ago.

programme.title: Secrets of the Queen's Coronation
programme.desc: A behind-the-scenes look at Queen Elizabeth II's coronation. This documentary shares memories and anecdotes from people who plays key roles at the celebration, all speaking publicly for the first time.

programme.title: Ren Faire
programme.desc: Jeff gains the upper hand over Louie. Darla's promotion to co-general manager leads to disastrous consequences for both Jeff and herself. George finally lands on the only successor he can trust.

programme.title: Santa Camp
programme.desc: Every summer, a horde of professional Santas, Mrs Clauses and elves descend on a campsite in the New Hampshire woods to learn the tricks of their trade. But this year is different. The organizers, members of the one-hundred strong New England Santa Society, have decided to tackle a complicated and historic problem: the lack of diversity in the Santa industry. They decide to enlist a Black Santa named Chris, a Santa with a disability named Fin, and a transgender Santa named Levi, each with their own surprising Santa origin story. Meanwhile, the Mrs Claus delegation, led by veteran Dianne, is becoming more outspoken in its calls for equal billing and pay. The arrival of the new Santas sparks a reckoning about what it means to embody the beloved role model and cultural icon. After graduation, the Clauses draw on their camp experiences to affect change in their communities. But they soon learn that not everybody is ready for Santas who "break the mold".

programme.title: Portrait Artist of the Year
programme.desc: Having made it through the semi-final, just three artists remain in the running for Portrait Artist of the Year 2020. Artists have four hours to create their portraits, and the hallowed halls of the National Portrait Gallery provide a fitting backdrop for their final sitter, renowned poet, Lemn Sissay. The artists also unveil their commission paintings of micro-sculptor Willard Wigan; landscape architect Kim Wilkie and chef Nieves Barragan Mohacho.

programme.title: The Brief History Of Cricket Downunder
programme.desc: By the early 90s, players excel in one-day cricket, becoming specialists who transform the game and its dynamics.

programme.title: The Australian Dream: Adam Goodes
programme.desc: The Australian Dream is a documentary that uses the remarkable and inspirational story of Indigenous AFL legend Adam Goodes as the prism through which to tell a deeper and more powerful story about race, identity and belonging. The film will unpick the events of the 2013-15 AFL seasons and ask fundamental questions about the nature of racism and discrimination in society today. Walkley award-winning writer Stan Grant and BAFTA award-winning director Daniel Gordon join forces to tell this remarkable story of one of the most decorated and celebrated players in AFL history. A man who remains a cultural hero; the very epitome of resilience and survival, who continues to fight for equality and reconciliation.

programme.title: The Day of the Jackal
programme.desc: It is the early 1960s in France. The remaining survivors of the aborted French Foreign Legion have made repeated attempts to kill President DeGaulle. The result is that he is the most closely guarded man in the world. As a desperate act, they hire The Jackal, the codename for a hired killer who agrees to kill the French president for half a million dollars. We watch his preparations, which are so thorough we wonder how he could possibly fail, even as we watch the French police attempt to pick up his trail.

programme.desc: This episode sees Noel, Sandi, Prue and Paul welcoming brand-new bakers to the festive tent for the first time, in the form of the Derry Girls star cast.

programme.title: Gordon Ramsay's Ultimate Home Cooking
programme.desc: Gordon goes full-on to produce great hearty food that packs a deliciously robust punch. Recipes guaranteed to keep you cosy, warm and wonderfully well fed are superb Welsh rarebit and delectable roasted tomato soup.

programme.title: Jamie's 15 Minute Meals
programme.desc: Jamie's rustling up beef stroganoff, fluffy rice, red onion and parsley pickle, plus Moroccan mussels, tapenade toasties and cucumber salad.

programme.title: Mary Berry Classic
programme.desc: Mary returns to one of her most loved locations, Port Isaac on the Cornwall coast. Along with the brilliant local Chef Nathan Outlaw she explores some of the wonderful classics inspired by the great outdoors - even if the weather is against them! From her take on a delicious Bouillabaisse filled with some of the ocean's best offerings, to a freshly caught Mackerel bbq'd by the sea, and some rich and crispy Spatchcock Poussin marinated and cooked alongside it. As the home cook and Michelin started chef cook alongside each other it is obvious how inspired they both are by the coast and a shared love of simple, fresh, local food cooked well.

programme.title: Jamie's Cracking Christmas
programme.desc: Time to deck those halls and spread a bit of Jamie's festive foodie cheer. It's a time to indulge in some of the most special food you'll eat all year.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie celebrates leftovers and transforms them into delicious dishes for between Christmas and New Year: a vegetable tarte tatin, Brussels sprouts Caesar salad, and a spectacular trifle.

programme.title: Nigella's Christmas Special
programme.desc: Nigella shows us how to have our most relaxed, most spectacular Christmas yet with her foolproof guide to stress-free festive cooking and easy entertaining.

programme.title: Mary Berry's Ultimate Christmas
programme.desc: Much loved British home cook Mary Berry is joined by some famous friends for her ultimate Christmas feast, packed with tips to plan and get ahead for the big day.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas With Bells On
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver provides an inspiring collection of traditional Christmas classics, alongside new ideas and fantastic party food.

programme.title: Eat Well For Less NZ
programme.desc: A Nana has taken on three grandchildren all with very different dietary demands. Michael van de Elzen and restauranteur Ganesh Raj are on the case to get this family to eat the same meal around a table.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Shortcuts
programme.desc: Jamie celebrates leftovers and transforms them into delicious dishes for between Christmas and New Year: a vegetable tarte tatin, Brussels sprouts Caesar salad, and a spectacular trifle.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Today, reputations are on the line, as 3 of our cake makers aim to impress at the world's most prestigious cake show, Cake International. Former tattoo artist Ben Cullen, battles to make a life sized Ringmaster Cake, standing over 6ft high, and covered in a whopping 15 kilos of detailed sugar paste. Molly hopes to make a splash with her 6ft square seascape, complete with a Mermaid covered in over 600 edible handmade scales. It's a race against time as Phil and Christine paint the final one of 5 famous works of art using cocoa butter on sugar paste live at the show, to complete their Sugar Art Gallery.

programme.title: Nigella's Christmas Special
programme.desc: Nigella shows us how to have our most relaxed, most spectacular Christmas yet with her foolproof guide to stress-free festive cooking and easy entertaining.

programme.title: Gordon, Gino & Fred Desperately Seeking
programme.desc: Gordon, Gino and Fred are getting together for another extraordinary culinary Christmas adventure. 2020 has been a bit bleak to say the least and the boys are keen to end the year with a bang. After Gino led them to the deserts of Morocco for last year's mis-adventure, Gordon is determined that they celebrate the season properly this time, so has arranged a boys trip away before the madness of a family xmas kicks in, to the ultimate winter wonderland and the official home of Santa. GGF are going to Lapland to experience a treasure trove of festive fare, to enjoy spectacular landscapes and meet Father Christmas in person. Standby for a unique Christmas cracker!

programme.title: The Great Australian Bake Off

programme.title: Jeremy Pang's Asian Kitchen

programme.title: Justine Schofield Queensland Gourmet
programme.desc: In order to make her smoky eggplant and cocoa dip, Justine sources some local cocoa in Mossman. At the Witches Chase, she makes an omelette with three types of cheese.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers Christmas Special
programme.desc: Christmas just wouldn't be the same without cakes, and as the decorations go up, 6 of our Extreme Cake Makers are hard at work pushing their creations to new seasonal sugary highs! Ben has a battle to create a 3-foot tall Naughty Elf Cake in time to delight revellers and dignitaries at Chester's Christmas Lights Parade. Hand carved from chocolate cake and sugar paste, it weighs in at a staggering 30 kilos. Molly must tackle not one but three animal firsts, as she sculpts a lemur, meerkat, and armadillo, in the hope of creating the perfect festive bake to celebrate Santa's arrival at an Exotic Zoo. Suzanne flexes her sugar crafting and story writing skills as she creates Father Christmas reading a gingerbread book, covered in 3D modelling chocolate figures, that tells the tale of how Santa got his first red coat.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: It's another challenging day for our extreme cake makers as they make a high-flying show biz sponge, a Remembrance Day centrepiece, and a cake take on an iconic military vehicle. Former army helicopter pilot Rosie, faces her most precarious cake build to date, as she attempts to suspend a life-sized edible figure over the heads of the cast of a West End musical. Covered in 60 kilos of fondant, the clothing and facial features must match the show's leading man. Suzanne must impress with a detailed cake replica of the Lloyd's of London's Lutine Bell- which forms the focal point for a Remembrance Day service. Taking 130 hours to create, it includes 75 sugar poppies that look like they are falling around the cake. Ben battles to make a cake replica of a tank, good enough to astound tank enthusiast Tommy-Lee on his 18th birthday.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Former tattoo artist turned cake maker Ben Cullen feels the pressure as he's asked to make a caricature of a cake maker for this 50th birthday party. Standing over 2 ft tall, and dressed in a black and white sugar paste tuxedo, it's intricately carved to catch the birthday boy's cheeky character. Nastassja tackles an unconventional 5th wedding anniversary cake for a couple who want a work of art, rather than a traditional cake. Built from 5 huge see-through chocolate cake triangles, it's hand decorated with hundreds of edible paper strips. Meanwhile, show biz comes to Penzance, as Phil and Christine plan a mechanical masterpiece, with a 5ft wide glittery lorry cake, complete with rotating edible kinky boots for a musicals first night party.

programme.title: Jamie's Quick And Easy Christmas
programme.desc: Jamie applies his quick and easy principles to the festive season, focusing on genius combinations of just 5 ingredients to create most fabulous Christmas feast. Jamie's Quick & Easy Christmas is brilliantly simple and delivers utterly delicious results with all of the flavour but none of the fuss. It's the easy way to an impressive Christmas. This year, Jamie is cooking up his Quick & Easy Christmas at home, creating 10 brand new festive recipes for his family and close friends, and joined in the kitchen from time to time by his very little helpers: Petal, Buddy and River,

programme.title: Jamie At Home Christmas
programme.desc: In this special, Jamie takes the strain out of Christmas dinner and allows you to reclaim your Christmas day. By following Jamie's advice you can be calm, organised and prepared and your Christmas dinner will be a sure-fire winner. Good ingredients are paramount and Jamie has a visit from his geese and turkey suppliers who advise us what to look for in a good bird. Jamie and Brian, his gardener, look at the greens on offer in the garden and Gennaro, Jamie's Italian mentor, drops in to roast some chestnuts. Jamie walks us through the elements of the perfect roast dinner and tips on how to prepare and cook your birds, how to roast the perfect potatoes, dress the perfect greens and make sensational mince pies. By following Jamie's advice and tips your Christmas culinary preparations should be stress-free forever more.

programme.title: Jamie's Christmas Cookbook
programme.desc: Jamie Oliver has been cooking Christmas for his family for 20 years. In that time, he's experimented with loads of different ways of cooking the classics. These are the dishes he'll be cooking on the big day for his own family this year.

programme.title: Paul Hollywood Eats Japan
programme.desc: Paul's spending his first week in Japan's mega city capital, Tokyo. He has a lesson in Japanese restaurant etiquette, sees Godzilla and visits one of the worlds cheapest Michelin starred restaurants. He sniffs out an extraordinary bakery which puts a very big smile on his face, then samples the Japanese practice of solo dining and the even weirder practice of solo Karaoke. Finally, Paul heads off on a karting tour of the city with a man called Lady Beard. After fishing for their lunch, they cuddle some puppies, risk life and limb on the streets of Tokyo and finish Paul's first week with a three-course dinner up the posh end of a jumbo jet well, sort of.

programme.title: Extreme Cake Makers
programme.desc: Today, cake artist Michelle's cake world almost comes crashing down as she tackles a 3ft wide rotating planet earth cake to celebrate the 40th anniversary of a travel guide. With the cake needing to appear at two parties, this globe includes 2 identical vanilla cake tops so it can be cut twice, and the planet's surface is covered in 20 kilos of accurately detailed royal icing. Phil and Christine must miniaturise their skills, as they tackle their smallest wedding cake to date for a couple who've eloped to Cornwall to get married. Consisting of 2 tiny Mr and Mrs cakes made in their favourite flavours of chocolate fudge and lemon drizzle, they're surrounded by a 'moon gate' of sugar flowers and each one is decorated to match their wedding outfits. Molly makes a spectacular life-like, regal leopard cake, complete with a sugar paste crown, to help raise money at a charity auction.

programme.desc: An unexpected issue arises on Sam and Tricia's trip to Kansas City for a trade show. Joel reluctantly attends Brad's bible book club and runs into a complicated figure from his past.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Surrounded by her sister and chosen family on Thanksgiving, Sam gains a better appreciation of what it means to love.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.sub-title: As Much As I Like Not Feeling?
programme.desc: Joel finds himself feeling unstable after an accident; Sam realises her deepest insecurities as she goes on a date with Iceland.

programme.title: Somebody Somewhere
programme.desc: Sam always feels like an outsider in her hometown Kansas. However, she later finds a community where people like her gather amidst their struggles in life.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Attending an elaborate launch party, Richard and his computer programmer friends, Big Head, Dinesh and Gilfoyle, dream of making it big. Instead, they're living in a communal Hacker Hostel.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Peter Gregory demands a formal business plan for Pied Piper. Meanwhile Richard hires Jared, a former Hooli employee and they set about trying to trim the fat.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: While Gavin Belson begins to hype Nucleus, a competing compression platform, Richard learns that the name Pied Piper is already registered to a sprinkler company, forcing him to negotiate.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: At a toga party thrown by Peter, Richard drunkenly promises to make Erlich a board member - a decision he regrets the next morning.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich convinces a graffiti artist to create Pied Pipers logo, with controversial results, and Jared tries to make the company more efficient.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard feels threatened when the team hires The Carver, a hacker with a notorious reputation, to help with Pied Piper's cloud.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: At TechCrunch Disrupt, Richard is distracted by a girl he dated briefly, and Erlich's connection with a judge threatens Pied Piper.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Poised to compete at TechCrunch Disrupt, the guys of Pied Piper become worried after an impressive presentation by Gavin Belson.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: After taking the tech world by storm at TechCrunch Disrupt, Richard and his Pied Piper team look ahead to a bright future, but are immediately soured by petty revenge plans from arch rival, Nucleus.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and the guys struggle to find a backer as they face legal and financial woes. Jared uses the BRO2BRO app to try and help get them out of their mess.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard mulls Gavin's proposal, but also considers a pitch from billionaire Russ Hanneman. Monica learns unexpected news about Richard's deal with Hooli, and Gilfoyle and Dinesh go to extremes to get what they want.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and Erlich butt heads over a potential employee. Dinesh and Gilfoyle become suspicious that someone they recommended for the company will be receiving more money than them.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Gavin's interference causes Pied Piper to struggle with securing their expansion, a neighbour threatens the team's security, and Dinesh and Gilfoyle are again questioning whether to embark on a solo mission.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper crew races to show up Hooli after Nucleus' UFC livestream debut is botched. Erlich introduces them to the godfather of Homicide energy drink and former "mentee" Double-A to get some PR advice.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Russ becomes distracted by financial news and Richard faces the prospect of folding. Meanwhile, Dinesh tries wooing a woman online and Gavin revels in Nucleus' failure.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard causes trouble when he sympathises with a rival, making him paranoid about security. Meanwhile, Pied Piper, Gavin, and Intersite all face massive losses, undoing any progress made in recent weeks.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich wants to testify when Pied Piper and Hooli enter a negotiation, but Richard worries that his rival's claims could be legitimate. Jared, Dinesh and Gilfoyle debate a philosophical theory.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The guys wait anxiously to hear whether it's life or death for Pied Piper. Meanwhile, Erlich sends a message to the real estate buyers and Dinesh's incompetent coding works out better than expected.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: After being unceremoniously fired, an angry Richard faces a tough decision: whether to accept the diminished role of CTO, or leave Pied Piper behind for good. Meanwhile Erlich takes a shine to Lauries new choice of CEO, Jack Barker, while Dinesh and Gilfoyle weigh their options in Richard's absence. At Hooli, Gavin tries to improve his image by admitting failure, and Big Head gets wind of some major changes.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: While Pied Piper's new and improved offices and perks impress Dinesh and Gilfoyle, Richard worries that his vision has been compromised, especially after hearing from Jack's newly-hired sales team. Jared faces issues evicting renters from his condo, while a frustrated Erlich struggles to get rid of Jian-Yang. Gavin suggests a controversial move at Hooli.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Facing the bleak future of Pied Piper's new business agenda, Richard tries to appeal to Laurie for help. Gilfoyle opens himself up to recruiters' pursuits. Dinesh's latest flashy purchase brings him unwanted attention. After stumbling upon information about the competition, the guys decide to work up a master plan to covertly force Jack's hand.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Forced to complete an uninspiring project, the guys struggle to perform at a lesser level, ending up with a product that's more successful than they intended. Erlich discovers he's facing Hacker Hostel competition from a familiar face; Monica takes a stand at a board meeting. Convinced of Pied Piper's threat, Gavin makes a decision to double down on Nucleus.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Nervous about Laurie's next steps, Richard takes matters into his own hands, letting his ego get the better of him during an interview. While cleaning house in an attempt to cut costs, Dinesh, Gilfoyle and Jared misplace a very important piece of hardware. Laurie reveals her true intentions after inviting Monica out for a drink. Erlich faces unexpected pushback while trying to sell Big Head on his plans for their future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard finds himself in his element while leading a global staff of engineers, but his budding relationship is threatened by his coding neuroses. Dinesh takes an interest in a foreign coworker, but worries about a face-to-face meeting. Big Head and Erlich's launch party hits a snag, or two, while Jared returns to the dating scene.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: When the guys decide to release the beta version of Pied Piper, they receive an unexpected response. With a limited number of invites each, Dinesh worries about his lack of friends.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Erlich struggles to come clean to Richard, who is forced to make a choice between their friendship and the company's future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Upon discovering surprising stats, Richard attempts to bridge the gap between Pied Piper and its users, leading Jared to take drastic measures to hold everything together.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: With Pied Piper's future in question, Erlich's publicity success leaves Richard in a moral quandary, just as Dinesh's video-chat app gathers speed.

programme.desc: Dinesh and Richard butt heads over PiperChat, Erlich's interest is piqued by Jian-Yang's new app, and Gavin grows paranoid.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard works tirelessly on his next big idea; Monica sets a trap; Dinesh goes on a date; Big Head heads back to school.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard's new alliance worries Jared, Gilfoyle considers whether to put his pride aside, and Erlich confronts Jian-Yang about his app.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard deals with an unexpected interloper; Dinesh searches for a way out; Monica considers her options.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard looks outside the tech bubble for support, Erlich searches for a new endeavour, and Jared tries to keep the peace between Dinesh and Gilfoyle.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard takes on a patent troll; Gilfoyle battles a smart fridge; Jared embraces multiple identities; Erlich tries to hang with a new crowd.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard ponders a deal with the tech world's latest 'it' boy, while Jack faces setbacks.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper guys try to pull off a stealth plan at Hooli-Con; Erlich takes a trip to get his mojo back.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard finds himself in a web of lies; Jared plans his exit; Jack bets big; Gavin plots a comeback.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard faces new challenges as a leader as Pied Piper levels up, and Gavin worries about being antiquated.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard faces challenges while trying to unite new employees, Jian-Yang goes to court, and Gavin is questioned over his signature.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard's infatuation with another company's COO causes friction with members of Pied Piper, and Dinesh gets a new roommate.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Attempting to woo a gaming company to PiperNet, Richard inadvertently angers one of his prized allies. Dinesh deals with a betrayal. In preparation for a big launch, Gavin leaves his underlings with a cryptic message. Jared gets inside information from Big Head.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Overshadowed by Jared in an on-camera interview, Richard's confidence wavers further when Laurie and Monica force him to work with a new artificial-intelligence company, Eklow. Gilfoyle worries about the prospect of introducing AI into Pied Piper. Dinesh makes Jared self-conscious ahead of a second interview. Gavin questions his future beyond Hooli.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard decides to help when Laurie gets in a jam, but his lack of emotional discipline threatens to backfire on Pied Piper. Gavin tries to make a deal with a stubborn partner while abroad. Dinesh relishes a rare win. Jared bonds with a surprising figure.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: As the Pied Piper guys prepare to close on their Series B funding, Richard receives unsettling news. Dinesh competes with a co-worker, while Jared keeps a close watch on Richard's new assistant.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: PiperNet finds Monica suspicious of an early success, and the team must race against the clock as their future is threatened. Realising he's made more enemies than friends, Richard makes a surprising move.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard discovers his promise to keep Pied Piper free from collecting user data is under threat, while Jared finds himself missing his role as Richard's go-to guy.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard meets a potential investor, while Gilfoyle butts heads with HR. Gavin explores a leaner Hooli's future.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: The Pied Piper team races to close a major deal, while Dinesh considers being a better person. An angry Jared reluctantly helps Richard.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: When Richard's authority is threatened by his former Hooli manager, Dinesh has just the solution. Jared learns some hard truths about his birth parents.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard fumes at Gavin's new ethical stance, Dinesh's trip turns into a nightmare, while Gilfoyle and Monica work on their peer review scores.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard, Dinesh, Gilfoyle and Jared face their greatest challenge yet as a massive event tests PiperNet's capabilities, while Laurie's unexpected presence makes them wary of sabotage.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and the Pied Piper guys finally see the fruits of their labour, but realise that a bigger company means much bigger problems.

programme.title: Silicon Valley
programme.desc: Richard and the Pied Piper guys finally see the fruits of their labour, but realise that a bigger company means much bigger problems.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: It is two years earlier: in Camp Toccoa, GA, volunteers train for America's new military experiment, the paratroops. Theirs is one of nine companies in the 506th Parachute Infantry Regiment: E, aka Easy Company, commanded by the domineering Lt. Herbert Sobel. Through a regimen that involves frequent six-mile climbs up and down Currahee, a local mountain, Sobel succeeds in drilling the men into an elite unit. But his heavy-handed tactics, punishments for petty or imagined infractions, and strategic failures during manoeuvres, alienate Sobel from his men, who prefer his cool-headed second-in-command, Lt. Winters. After the 506th is shipped to England to complete training, Sobel harasses Winters by citing him for failing to inspect a latrine on time. When Winters requests a trial by court-martial, six of his sergeants: Salty Harris, Chuck Grant, Bill Guarnere, Carwood Lipton, Mike Ranney and Floyd Talbert request transfer rather than follow Sobel into combat.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Landing in a field, Winters finds he has lost his rifle and supply bag. He teams up with a private from another company, John Hall, and sets out to find their units. During the night they link up with a few more soldiers, and ambush a horse-drawn German supply convoy, their first enemy encounter. Regrouping in a nearby town, Winters runs into Easy's Lt. 'Buck' Compton, who tells him that Lt. Meehan is unaccounted for, which puts Winters in charge. His orders are to lead an attack on a nearby cluster of German artillery pieces endangering the seaborne infantry at Utah Beach. Winters deploys a small group on the entrenched enemy positions, and eventually takes four artillery pieces in succession, disabling them with TNT. The euphoria is tempered, however, when Winters finds Hall dead, killed by machine-gun fire. The mission is successful, but Winters has lost his first man as acting company commander.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Harry Welsh delivers Easy's next orders: take Carentan, the only town where armoured vehicles from Omaha and Utah beaches can link up. Winters briefs the men, and Easy heads into Carentan, encountering fierce resistance from Germans holed up in buildings. The town is eventually secured, although Winters gets hit above his ankle by ricochet gunfire.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: The respite doesn't last long; in September the men learn they're being airlifted to Holland as part of Operation Market-Garden, a joint American-British offensive that aims at securing quick access to Germany and ending the war by Christmas. Unlike Normandy, the daytime parachute drop is executed smoothly, and the company is greeted as heroes as they enter the town of Eindhoven after minimal resistance. All that changes when the company marches to Nuenen, where Easy is met by a brutal attack that ends with Compton wounded, a retreat ordered, and 'Bull' Randleman stranded in the town overnight. It's a sobering and bloody initiation to combat for the replacements.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As Winters labours over a report on the dike mission, Heyliger leads a rescue of British soldiers escaping from the besieged town of Arnhem. An anxious Winters worries about Easy (now one of three companies he helps command), especially after Heyliger is shot and wounded by a nervous sentry. After moving off the line to France, Nixon insists that Winters take a break and see Paris. Winters is haunted there by the memory of a young German he killed at close range on the dike. As he returns to the Company at Mourmelon, news comes of a massive German counterattack in the Ardennes Forest. Winters helps Easy race there to hold the line, his men ill-equipped for the cold and the battle ahead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Medic Eugene Roe is overwhelmed, on edge and close to combat exhaustion, when he finds friendship with a Belgian nurse. Easy Company spends a miserable Christmas in the trenches, but is buoyed after receiving the news that the German army's demand for surrender was met with General McAuliffe's defiant answer: "Nuts!"

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Several are killed and wounded in fierce shelling, compounded by the incompetence of their new commander, Lieutenant Dike, about whom Winters can do nothing. Easy takes Foy, but at an enormous cost.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Hank Jones, fresh from West Point and eager for combat experience, volunteers to lead, though he must convince a sceptical Winters. Also assigned to the patrol is Private David Webster, back in Easy after rehabilitation of an injury. While successful, the mission costs a soldier's life, prompting Winters to ignore the order to send out a second patrol the next night.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: A patrol in a nearby forest discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp, still filled with emaciated prisoners. The local citizenry, unbelievably disavowing knowledge of its existence, is made to clean it up. Suddenly, news arrives from Berlin: Hitler is dead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.title: Band of Brothers

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Medic Eugene Roe is overwhelmed, on edge and close to combat exhaustion, when he finds friendship with a Belgian nurse. Easy Company spends a miserable Christmas in the trenches, but is buoyed after receiving the news that the German army's demand for surrender was met with General McAuliffe's defiant answer: "Nuts!"

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Several are killed and wounded in fierce shelling, compounded by the incompetence of their new commander, Lieutenant Dike, about whom Winters can do nothing. Easy takes Foy, but at an enormous cost.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: Lt. Hank Jones, fresh from West Point and eager for combat experience, volunteers to lead, though he must convince a sceptical Winters. Also assigned to the patrol is Private David Webster, back in Easy after rehabilitation of an injury. While successful, the mission costs a soldier's life, prompting Winters to ignore the order to send out a second patrol the next night.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: A patrol in a nearby forest discovers an abandoned Nazi concentration camp, still filled with emaciated prisoners. The local citizenry, unbelievably disavowing knowledge of its existence, is made to clean it up. Suddenly, news arrives from Berlin: Hitler is dead.

programme.title: Band of Brothers
programme.desc: As German officers hand over their weapons, soldiers raid well-stocked wine cellars, snag souvenirs from residences, and endure one final tragedy when a replacement soldier cracks. Despite the fact that VE (Victory in Europe) Day has passed, Easy faces the prospect of imminent deployment to the Pacific Theatre. But as the men compare "points" to see who has earned enough to go home, word arrives that the Japanese have surrendered, ending the war and dissolving Easy Company. A memorable closing vignette tells what happened to Winters and some of his men after they returned to the States.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: In the immediate aftermath of the Japanese attack on Pearl Harbor, Marine Sgt John Basilone prepares to ship out and confront the enemy somewhere in the Pacific, while budding journalist Robert Leckie enlists in the Marine Corps. Eugene Sledge, unable to enlist because of a heart murmur, says farewell to his best friend, Sidney Phillips, who is about to leave for boot camp. Exactly eight months after Pearl Harbor, the 1st Marine Division, including Leckie and Phillips, lands on Guadalcanal in order to secure its strategically vital airfield and prepare for the inevitable counterattack.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Basilone and the 7th Marines arrive on Guadalcanal to reinforce Leckie and the rest of the 1st Marine Division as they continue to defend the crucial airstrip. Basilone plays a key role in repelling a night time Japanese attack, but suffers a frightful personal loss. After four months of continuous action, the exhausted and disease-ridden members of the 1st Marine Division are evacuated off the island.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Physically and mentally debilitated after the four-month ordeal on Guadalcanal, Leckie, Basilone and thousands of their comrades land in Melbourne, where they are greeted by adoring crowds and viewed as the saviours of Australia. While his buddies carouse, Leckie becomes deeply attached to an Australian woman and her first-generation Greek family. Meanwhile, Basilone is awarded the Medal of Honour and is asked to return home to help sell US war bonds.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Finally enlisted as a marine, Sledge trains for combat at Camp Elliott. The 1st Marine Division lands at Cape Gloucester on the Japanese-held island of New Britain. As Leckie and the other marines battle the Japanese, they quickly realise that the more ominous enemy is the smothering jungle itself. Having survived Gloucester and being stationed on the godforsaken island of Pavuvu, Leckie begins displaying the physical and mental effects of combat and is sent to a naval hospital on nearby Banika for psychiatric observation.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Basilone's celebrity grows as he travels across the country on the war bonds tour. On Pavuvu, Sledge, assigned to the 5th Marines, 1st Marine Division, is briefly reunited with Phillips and Leckie rejoins his company. Sledge then gets his first taste of combat as he, Leckie, and the rest of the 1st Marine Division meet fierce Japanese resistance while landing on the intricately and heavily defended coral island of Peleliu.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Despite the suffocating 115-degree heat and a lack of clean drinking water, Sledge, Leckie and the other Marines confront the highly-fortified enemy as they attempt to capture the Peleliu airfield. After seeing his comrades badly injured, Leckie himself is wounded and evacuated from the island. Sledge witnesses the shocking truth about what is sometimes required to survive and fight another day.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: The Marines, including the newly christened 'Sledgehammer', continue the battle of Peleliu against an enemy determined to fight to the last man. Devastated by the loss of a revered leader, and witnessing unimaginable barbarity on both sides, Sledge veers to the very edge of moral collapse. Their objective finally secured, the Marines return to Pavuvu fundamentally changed by their experience on Peleliu.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: Increasingly frustrated by his role campaigning for war bonds, Basilone convinces the marines to allow him to train troops headed for combat. Transferred to Camp Pendleton, he enjoys a whirlwind romance with an initially reluctant female marine, Lena Riggi. But the couple know they are living on borrowed time, as Basilone is soon to take part in the marine-landing on Iwo Jima.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: After battling across the island of Okinawa for over a month, Sledge and the rest of the 1st Marine Division are ordered to relieve an Army division that has been in combat against the most strongly defended Japanese position on the island. The primordial conditions and the moral dilemma posed by the presence of civilians puts tremendous strain on the physical and psychological endurance of Sledge and the other Marines.

programme.title: The Pacific
programme.desc: After the Japanese surrender, Leckie, healed from his wounds, leaves the hospital and returns home, while Sledge heads back to Alabama to be reunited with his family and Sid Phillips. Lena visits Basilone's home and has an emotional meeting with his family. Leckie adjusts to post-war life by resuming his old job and starting a new relationship, but for Sledge, unsure why he survived the war seemingly unscathed, adjustment will require more time.
